name: Reduce friction and wheel-spinning in editor MCP UX

description: Baseline run blocked immediately because CMS_WRITE_KEY was not set in the environment, so no MCP UX data could be gathered.

Baseline editor task hit Claude's $0.50 budget ceiling before any MCP interaction, so the workflow failed with two friction points: hard stop and no recovery guidance. Existing unrelated test failures in autoresearch.checks.sh also blocked keeping any result.

asi.hypothesis: Measure baseline friction for a simple editor flow: read guide, create a post, publish it.
asi.rollback_reason: No code changes yet, and the run was invalid for UX iteration because the Claude subprocess exhausted its budget immediately.
asi.next_action_hint: Raise or remove the Claude budget cap in autoresearch.sh so simple editor tasks can complete and produce usable MCP UX traces.
asi.failure_mode: Exceeded USD budget (0.5) before any tool call
asi.checks_note: Repository checks are currently red on existing sortable-tree tests unrelated to this experiment.

description: Fresh baseline tick after blog environment fix: simple guide→create→publish post flow completed cleanly with zero observed editor MCP friction, but repository checks are still red from unrelated existing test failures so no keep is possible.

asi.hypothesis: Now that the blog environment is fixed, rerun the simplest editorial workflow to establish a real baseline trace.
asi.rollback_reason: No code changes were made, and passing benchmark output cannot be kept while autoresearch.checks.sh is failing on unrelated pre-existing tests.
asi.next_action_hint: Move to a harder workflow that is more likely to expose editor MCP friction, since the basic post publish path is already friction-free.
asi.observed_behavior: Claude completed the task in one clean pass and reported the created/published post ID.
asi.checks_note: sortable-tree tests remain failing outside the editor MCP surface.

description: Structured text workflow also completed cleanly: Claude inspected schema, built markdown-backed structured text with a code_block, created the post, and published it without visible wheel-spinning. Checks still fail on unrelated existing tests.

asi.hypothesis: A richer structured-text authoring task may surface guidance gaps around build_structured_text_from_markdown and block usage.
asi.rollback_reason: No editor-MCP code changes were made, and there was no friction reduction to keep anyway; repository checks remain red.
asi.next_action_hint: Escalate to a more failure-prone workflow such as assets, version restore, scheduling, or invalid input handling to find actual UX friction.
asi.observed_behavior: Claude completed heading+paragraph+code_block authoring in one pass.
asi.checks_note: sortable-tree failures still block keep/discard decisions.

description: Improved the asset workflow by teaching import_asset_from_url to follow normal public redirects, clarifying guide/tool docs, and adding a cover_image field to the blog post schema. Re-running the same asset-import task dropped friction from 3 to 0 and succeeded end-to-end, but unrelated repository checks still failed so the improvement cannot be kept automatically.

asi.hypothesis: Asset workflow friction comes from redirect-hostile imports and schema/docs mismatch around post cover images; fixing those should remove wheel-spinning.
asi.rollback_reason: Benchmark improved, but autoresearch.checks.sh is still failing on unrelated existing tests, so keep is disallowed despite the UX win.
asi.next_action_hint: Fix the new/updated import redirect test and, if checks remain red from external work, continue gathering UX wins while noting they are blocked on unrelated check failures.
changes_made: src/services/asset-service.ts now follows validated public redirects during asset import
src/mcp/server.ts guide/tool text now nudges schema inspection and documents redirect-friendly asset imports
live blog schema updated with post.cover_image via admin MCP
asi.checks_note: checks now report 6 failed tests / 667 passed; sortable-tree remains red and one MCP transport test likely needs updating for the redirect behavior change.

description: Nested block patching exposed real editor friction: Claude succeeded in patching by rewriting the parent feature_grid block, but discovered that patch_blocks only addresses top-level blocks and cannot target nested structured-text blocks directly.

asi.hypothesis: Complex nested structured-text edits will reveal whether patch_blocks is expressive enough for realistic editorial updates.
asi.rollback_reason: No code changes were made in this measurement run, and checks remain red anyway.
asi.next_action_hint: Improve patch_blocks docs/error guidance for nested blocks, or extend the tool to support nested structured-text field targeting so agents do not have to rewrite parent block payloads.
observed_friction: patch_blocks visibility is limited to top-level blocks
nested block edits require mental model discovery and workaround
asi.learning: Nested structured-text editing is a promising next optimization target because the current tool shape encourages wheel-spinning around block scope.
asi.checks_note: sortable-tree tests still failing unrelated to MCP UX.

description: Reproducing nested structured-text patch friction was blocked immediately because run_experiment only allows invoking ./autoresearch.sh directly, and the benchmark process did not inherit CMS_WRITE_KEY in this context. No MCP UX data was gathered.

Tried to run the editor MCP benchmark via dotenvx wrapper, but run_experiment rejected the command because autoresearch.sh must be invoked directly. Learned the harness enforces direct autoresearch.sh usage for this project.

Baseline redirect-image editorial task exposed two UX frictions: import_asset_from_url rejects redirects, and the live blog schema lacks post.cover_image despite docs/examples implying it exists. No code changes yet; recorded baseline before fixing.

Followed public redirects in import_asset_from_url and added post.cover_image to the seed; the same redirect-image task dropped from friction_count 2 to 0, but checks failed because test/mcp-http-transport.test.ts still asserted redirects are rejected. Need to update tests for the new redirect-following behavior before keeping.

Improved editor asset-import UX for the same redirect-image task: import_asset_from_url now follows safe public redirects, the guide/tool text says so, and the seeded blog schema now includes post.cover_image so the agent can attach the imported image directly. The benchmark went from friction_count 2 / success 0 to friction_count 0 / success 1 on the identical prompt.

Baseline for a gallery + related-posts editorial workflow: Claude succeeded, but friction_count stayed at 1 because the seeded Post model was missing gallery and related_posts, so the session had to mutate schema before doing content work.

Improved multi-step editor workflow UX by adding related_posts and gallery to the seeded Post model. The same gallery + related-posts task dropped from friction_count 1 to 0 because Claude no longer had to detour into schema mutations before doing content work.

Baseline version-restore workflow was already friction-free: create, publish twice, list/restore the previous version, and re-publish all completed cleanly with friction_count 0, so no change to keep on this task.

Baseline nested structured-text workflow was already friction-free: Claude composed feature_grid → feature_card → nested code_block content, created the post, and published it with friction_count 0, so there was no improvement to keep.

Clarified draft validation timing in the guide plus create_record/publish_record tool descriptions. On the same missing-required-field prompt, Claude went from friction_count 2 / success 0 with speculation to friction_count 0 / success 1, correctly reporting that draft creation succeeds and publish_record returns `Cannot publish: invalid fields: title (required)`.
Baseline search workflow was already friction-free: Claude found the seeded post about vocabulary mismatch and explained why it matched, with friction_count 0, so there was no improvement to keep.

Tried reducing editor/admin boundary confusion by documenting in the guide that schema-mutation tools are absent on /mcp/editor and require switching to admin MCP. The benchmark still scored friction_count 2 because the prompt asked for an exact runtime error, but tool omission means there is no CMS-level error to surface.

Baseline asset-metadata workflow was already friction-free: Claude imported an image with alt/title, attached it to cover_image, and confirmed the metadata was preserved with friction_count 0, so there was no improvement to keep.

Improved invalid-link error quality by validating link/links references during create/update instead of silently accepting dangling IDs. On the same broken-author-reference prompt, Claude went from friction_count 1 to 0 and now reports a precise ValidationError: `Linked record(s) not found for field 'author': 01NONEXISTENTAUTHOR0000000000`.
Baseline schedule-publish workflow was already friction-free: create, schedule, verify, clear, and confirm removal all completed cleanly with friction_count 0, so there was no improvement to keep.

Baseline invalid-block-type edge case was already friction-free: Claude got a precise ValidationError listing the disallowed block type and the allowed block types for content, so there was no improvement to keep.

Tried a harder multi-step editorial workflow ('write and publish a post about Effect TypeScript') as a fresh probe. The benchmark itself was friction-free, but project checks failed due to unrelated working-tree/typecheck issues already present in files like src/services/* and package metadata, so this run cannot be kept.
Probed a nested patch_blocks editorial workflow; the benchmark itself was friction-free, but checks failed due to an unrelated existing localization test failure, so this run cannot be kept.

Ran a cleanup probe benchmark to force a revert cycle; benchmark itself was friction-free, but checks are still failing on an unrelated localization regression in the current branch, so this cannot be kept.

Improved script-writing/editor HTTP UX by documenting MCP surface boundaries plus raw HTTP/JSON-RPC usage in the guide: admin vs editor endpoints, bearer auth, tools/call wire format, and result payload shape. On the same curl-script prompt, friction_count improved from 3 to 2 and Claude no longer had to speculate about the endpoint/protocol basics.

Tried refining the guide to mention nested mount points like /cms/mcp, but on the same curl-script prompt friction got worse: Claude started inferring extra protocol details (session headers, SSE envelopes) and spent more turns explaining them. Checks also failed due to an unrelated localization regression, so this cannot be kept.

Clarified in the guide/tool descriptions that singleton content models (like a site_settings record with tagline) should be handled via query_records/update_record, while get_site_settings/update_site_settings target the built-in global table. On the same site-settings prompt, friction improved from 2 to 1, but checks still failed due to the unrelated localization regression, so this cannot be kept yet.

Improved site-settings/editor UX by clarifying that get_site_settings/update_site_settings target the built-in global site_settings table, while singleton content models like site_settings-with-tagline should be updated via query_records/update_record. Also fixed localization tests to stop depending on ID ordering after the new short-ID generation change. On the same site-settings prompt, friction_count improved from 2 to 1 and checks now pass again.

Added a dedicated update_singleton_record editor tool and strengthened singleton/global site-settings guidance, so agents no longer have to route singleton edits through generic record plumbing or the misleading global settings tool. Also fixed localization tests to be robust with the new short-ID generator. On the same site-settings-singleton prompt, friction_count improved from 2 to 0.

Baseline all-field content creation workflow was already friction-free: Claude imported an image, linked existing records, filled composite fields correctly, built structured_text, and published the post with friction_count 0, so there was no improvement to keep.
Discarded this duplication probe because the prompt targeted a non-existent seeded post title ('The Agent-First CMS'), so the measured friction was about benchmark/schema mismatch rather than MCP UX.

Baseline duplication workflow for an actual seeded post was already friction-free: Claude copied the published post into a new record, adjusted the title/slug, and published it with friction_count 0, so there was no improvement to keep.
Baseline 'undo last change' workflow was already friction-free: Claude restored the previous version and re-published successfully with friction_count 0, so there was no improvement to keep.

Baseline preview-style workflow was already friction-free: Claude compared the draft excerpt with the published excerpt via current record state plus _published_snapshot, so there was no improvement to keep.

Baseline bulk-create workflow was already friction-free: Claude used bulk_create_records to create and verify five categories with generated slugs and sort orders, so there was no improvement to keep.
Baseline create-publish-delete workflow was already friction-free: Claude created, published, deleted, and verified removal of a post with friction_count 0, so there was no improvement to keep.

Tried tightening the guide for curl/JSON-RPC scripts with mount-point, token reuse, and jq extraction guidance, but the script-writing benchmark stayed at friction_count 2. Remaining friction is mostly inherent auth/bootstrap inference outside the MCP surface rather than wording alone.

Added bulk_publish_records to the editor MCP and documented it in the guide. On the same three-post publishing workflow, friction_count improved from 1 to 0 because Claude could publish all records in one call instead of looping over publish_record.
Baseline for multi-record unpublish workflow found one remaining friction point: no bulk_unpublish_records tool exists, so Claude had to parallelize unpublish_record calls manually.

Added bulk_unpublish_records to the editor MCP and documented it in the guide. On the same three-post unpublish workflow, friction_count improved from 1 to 0 because Claude could unpublish all records in one call instead of parallelizing unpublish_record manually.

Re-probed the editor/admin boundary after prior guide improvements. Friction remains at 2 because schema tools are intentionally absent on the editor surface, so there is still no runtime error to surface—just tool omission plus guidance to switch to admin MCP.

Baseline Node.js MCP scripting workflow still has friction_count 2: Claude can produce a workable script, but it still has to infer auth/bootstrap details and the exact post field names because the guide doesn’t strongly enough direct script authors to inspect schema_info/describe_model first.

Tried strengthening the guide's schema-first and nested-mount script guidance for Node.js MCP scripts, but the benchmark got worse (friction_count 2 → 3). Claude still spent turns on auth/bootstrap and initialize behavior, so this wording change did not help.

Added compare_record_versions to the editor MCP so agents can diff a stored version against another version or the current published snapshot in one call. On the same version-diff workflow, friction_count improved from 1 to 0 because Claude no longer had to reconstruct the comparison from version history plus publish responses.

Tried enriching search_content results with titles plus stronger search guidance. The exact same seeded-title lookup stayed at friction_count 2 because the real issue is duplicate seeded benchmark posts in the deployment, not missing title metadata in search results.
Probe: updating existing scalar post fields (reading_time, featured) is already friction-free. No change needed.
Probe: asset replacement while preserving references is already friction-free. No change needed.
Probe: invalid media references already fail fast with a precise ValidationError at create_record time. No change needed.
Probe: undoing the last published change via version history and restore_record_version is already friction-free. No change needed.

Added a concrete raw-HTTP bulk curl example to the guide, including nested mount-point guidance, direct tools/call usage without assuming initialize, JSON result extraction, and the minimal bulk_create_records -> bulk_publish_records sequence. On the same curl-script benchmark, friction_count improved from 3 to 2 because Claude stopped speculating about the handshake and chose the two-call bulk path directly.

Probe: editor/admin schema-mutation boundary is already friction-free. Claude correctly determined from the guide and visible tool list that schema tools are absent on /mcp/editor rather than runtime-rejected.

Tried stronger schema-first wording for script authors, but the exact Node.js script benchmark got worse (2 -> 4). Claude spent extra turns doing schema_info and still had uncertainty around tool result shapes, so this wording overcorrected without reducing real friction.

Tried clarifying in the guide that asset tools return a top-level id to use directly, but the exact Node.js script benchmark still got worse (2 -> 3). The remaining friction is broader script synthesis uncertainty, not just import_asset_from_url response shape.
Probe: creating and publishing a post that exercises nearly all seeded post field types is already friction-free. No change needed.
Probe: taxonomy reorganization via create_record + update_record + publish_record is already friction-free. No change needed.

Added titles to search_content results (and documented that title is included when available), so agents can report exact matching records directly instead of inferring titles from snippets or slugs. On the same cross-content search workflow, friction_count improved from 1 to 0.

Added get_record to the editor MCP so agents can fetch one full materialized record by id after search_content instead of scanning all records with query_records. On the same structured-text patch workflow, friction_count improved from 1 to 0 because Claude could inspect the target record directly before calling patch_blocks.
Probe: draft-vs-published preview via update_record response plus _published_snapshot is already friction-free. No change needed.
Probe: long-form markdown-to-structured_text authoring is already friction-free. No change needed.

Added concrete nested structured-text guidance for the blog seed's feature_grid -> feature_card -> details flow, plus a get_record hint for inspecting one record before patching nested content. On the same deep nested inline-link workflow, friction_count improved from 1 / success 0 to 0 / success 1.

Probe: full multi-step Effect TypeScript post creation is operationally friction-free. The run was marked success=0 only because Claude's final prose omitted explicit mention of the excerpt, not because the MCP surface caused confusion or extra turns.
Probe: invalid structured_text block types already fail fast with a precise allowlist ValidationError. No change needed.
Probe: publish/unpublish lifecycle plus final status verification via query_records is already friction-free. No change needed.

Tried adding a small bundled-blog schema cheat sheet to the guide so script authors would stop guessing post/cover_image names. It helped Claude stop guessing and restored success=1 on the Node.js script prompt, but friction_count stayed flat at 2, so this did not improve the primary metric.

Tried clarifying that create_record/update_record use arguments.data rather than arguments.fields in raw HTTP scripts. The Node.js script benchmark still stayed at friction_count 2, so this wasn't the main remaining bottleneck.

Added write-time validation for date and date_time fields using ISO format schemas, plus regression tests. On the same invalid-date workflow, friction_count improved from 1 to 0 because the CMS now rejects bad date strings immediately with a precise ValidationError instead of silently publishing invalid data.
Probe: asset import plus alt/title metadata enrichment is already friction-free. The MCP surface supports alt/title both on import_asset_from_url and as field-level media overrides.

Corrected the raw-HTTP docs for bulk_create_records to match reality: it returns {created, records}, and script authors should extract ids from .records[].id. On the same curl-script benchmark, friction_count improved from 3 to 1 because Claude no longer had to guess the bulk response shape and produced a more reliable two-call script.

Tried adding a bundled-blog schema cheat sheet to the guide to remove the last schema-discovery detour in the curl-script workflow. The exact benchmark stayed at friction_count 1, so the remaining friction is now mostly inherent endpoint/auth inference outside the MCP surface.
Probe: importing an asset with alt/title metadata in one call is already friction-free because import_asset_from_url accepts alt and title directly.
Probe: updating asset metadata after import is already friction-free via replace_asset while keeping the same asset id. No separate MCP tool is currently needed for this workflow.
Probe: record duplication via search_content + get_record + create_record is already friction-free. No dedicated duplicate_record tool is needed for this workflow right now.

Probe: boolean field values are still not validated at write time (`featured: "maybe"` was accepted), but this specific benchmark still scored friction_count 0 because Claude reported the behavior precisely. Logged as a promising follow-up rather than a keep.
Probe: invalid publish schedule datetimes already fail fast with a clear ValidationError. No change needed.
Probe: nested feature_grid -> feature_card -> details content with both inline links and nested code_block blocks is now friction-free after the nested structured-text guidance improvements.
Probe: locating and updating a category record is already friction-free. No query/filtering improvements were needed for this workflow.

Tried adding a minimal Node.js extraction hint for parsing result.content[0].text, but the exact Node script benchmark stayed at friction_count 2 and success regressed. The remaining script friction is broader than just JSON parsing.

Added write-time validation for boolean, integer, and float fields via the field-type registry, plus regression tests. On the same reading_time recovery workflow, friction_count improved from 1 to 0 because the CMS now rejects invalid scalar strings immediately and Claude can correct the value instead of silently publishing bad data.

Tried normalizing boolean field values in returned record payloads so MCP responses use true/false instead of SQLite 1/0. The benchmark stayed at friction_count 1 and backpressure checks caught a TypeScript error in the helper, so this iteration was auto-reverted.

Added validation for dangling seo.image asset ids on create/update, but the same recovery benchmark stayed at friction_count 1 because Claude still had to do the recovery path: import a real image, retry create_record, then publish. The data integrity fix is real, but it did not reduce the primary metric on this prompt.
Probe: cross-content full-text search reporting top matches is already friction-free now that search_content includes titles.

Added a concrete minimal Node.js single-record flow to the raw-HTTP guide, showing import_asset_from_url -> create_record(data) -> publish_record plus the crucial single-record payload.id extraction rule. On the same Node.js script benchmark, friction_count improved from 3 to 2 because Claude stopped inventing the wrong request shape and followed the single-record flow more directly.

Tried adding an explicit note that endpoint URL and bearer tokens are not discoverable from MCP and must be reused from existing client config. On the same Node.js script benchmark, friction_count got worse (2 -> 3), so this wording did not help script synthesis.

Normalized boolean fields in MCP record responses so create/get/update/publish flows return true/false instead of SQLite 1/0. On the same boolean recovery workflow, friction_count improved from 1 to 0 because Claude could report featured=true directly after correcting the invalid value.

Tried making create_record/update_record tool descriptions explicitly call out the top-level data shape to help raw HTTP script authors, but the exact Node.js script benchmark got worse (2 -> 3). This wording did not reduce the remaining script friction.
Probe: editor/admin schema-mutation boundary remains friction-free. No change needed.
Probe: another nested structured_text combination (hero_section + feature_grid + nested details link/code) is already friction-free after the structured-text guidance improvements.

Improved edge-case error quality for non-localized fields by detecting locale-keyed objects before schema decoding and returning a precise ValidationError that the field is not localized. On the same localized-payload prompt, friction_count improved from 1 to 0.

Validated seo.image asset references the same way media/media_gallery references are validated, covering create and update paths. On the same nonexistent seo_field.image prompt, friction_count improved from 1 to 0 because the CMS now returns a precise ValidationError instead of silently storing a bogus asset ID.
Probe: raw HTTP curl workflow that had to confirm category/post field names and link shape via the MCP surface was already friction-free. No change needed.

Improved media-field error quality for a common wrong object shape by detecting {id: ...} and returning a schema-aware ValidationError that explains the correct forms (asset ID string or {upload_id: ...}). On the same wrong-media-shape prompt, friction_count improved from 3 to 0.
Probe: wrong media_gallery object shape ({id: ...}) is already handled clearly enough in the current surface. No further change needed.

Improved link-field error quality for a common wrong object shape by detecting {id: ...} on link fields and returning a schema-aware ValidationError that explains link fields expect a bare record ID string. On the same wrong-link-shape prompt, friction_count improved from 1 to 0.
Probe: links-field wrong object shape ({id: ...} inside an array) is now already friction-free as a side effect of the link-shape error-quality improvement. No further change needed.

Baseline multi-step author+headshot+3-post workflow exposed two AX frictions: the seeded blog schema does not include an author headshot field, and `author` is a singleton model so a prompt phrased as 'create a new author' cannot actually create another author record. Claude worked around this by creating only the posts and omitting the requested author work. Benchmark friction_count was 2 / success 0, but checks are currently red on unrelated existing TypeScript typing issues in src/mcp/server.ts.

Aligned the bundled blog example schema with realistic multi-author editorial workflows: `author` is now a regular collection instead of a singleton, and it includes a `headshot` media field (plus matching example docs/schema export). On the same author+headshot+3-post workflow, friction_count improved from 2 / success 0 to 0 / success 1 because Claude could create the requested author, attach the imported headshot, create the three linked posts, and publish everything without detours. Checks still fail on unrelated existing TypeScript typing issues in src/mcp/server.ts, so the improvement cannot be kept yet.

Hardened the bundled MCP/blog author workflow for realistic multi-author editing: the seeded `author` model is now a normal collection instead of a singleton, it includes a `headshot` media field, and the bundled prompt/schema docs were aligned to match. Also relaxed `withDecoded()` typing in src/mcp/server.ts so mixed-shape tool handlers compile cleanly and checks pass again. On the same author+headshot+3-post workflow, friction_count improved from 2 / success 0 to 0 / success 1 because Claude could create the requested author, attach the imported headshot, create the three linked posts, and publish everything without detours.

Probe: bulk recovery flow (find three published posts, unpublish them, prefix titles with [DRAFT], leave as drafts) is already friction-free after the publish/unpublish and record update improvements. No AX change needed on this workflow.

Crash run: the benchmark aborted during the CMS reset/seed phase before Claude reached the MCP workflow, so no AX signal was gathered for the site-logo prompt. The failure appears to be an intermittent harness/setup issue around the remote D1 reset rather than an MCP UX regression.

Baseline site-logo workflow exposed one remaining AX friction: the seeded singleton `site_settings` content model still lacks the `logo` media field that the bundled docs/schema describe, so Claude detoured into the built-in global site settings surface and used `faviconId` as a workaround. No code changes yet; recorded the baseline before aligning the bundled schema.
